claims.claim_text: Finite-aperture HgCdTe transmission and photoluminescence mapping, spatial variation, and model-conditioned composition or thickness inference predate R04.
claims.scope_conditions: Claim is limited to the audited full texts and bounded source-access record.
claims.unsupported_stronger_version: R04 invented finite-aperture or spatially resolved HgCdTe mapping.

claims.claim_text: A finite linear measurement of a stationary spatial field reports covariance filtered by the complete measurement kernel.
claims.scope_conditions: Stationary field and declared linear normalized kernel; nonlinear observation operators are treated separately.
claims.unsupported_stronger_version: The filtering identity is new mathematics or proves a universal HgCdTe covariance law.

claims.claim_text: For the isotropic Gaussian benchmark, filtered variance obeys V(s)=A/(1+2s^2/xi^2).
claims.scope_conditions: Two-dimensional isotropic Gaussian material covariance and Gaussian probe benchmark.
claims.unsupported_stronger_version: All HgCdTe spatial fields follow this Gaussian law.

claims.claim_text: One scale cannot identify both point variance and correlation length; two scales estimate the Gaussian benchmark but do not test its covariance family.
claims.scope_conditions: Identifiability statement is conditional on the declared Gaussian variance-versus-scale observation model.
claims.unsupported_stronger_version: Two scales validate Gaussian covariance or independently determine a universal material law.

claims.claim_text: A common multiplicative scale-calibration error is exactly confounded with absolute correlation length, producing an irreducible absolute-length uncertainty floor.
claims.scope_conditions: Exact local identity and declared nonlinear posterior conditions; bounded absolute-length priors can break factorization.
claims.unsupported_stronger_version: Calibration uncertainty can be removed by adding repeats at the same uncalibrated scales.

claims.claim_text: Gaussian covariance requires reciprocal variance to be linear in squared probe scale, so a third scale provides the first exact family-closure residual.
claims.scope_conditions: Closure applies to the Gaussian covariance/Gaussian probe benchmark and requires calibrated scales and covariance-aware uncertainty.
claims.unsupported_stronger_version: Failure to reject Gaussian closure proves Gaussian covariance.

claims.claim_text: Forcing a misspecified Gaussian covariance model produces scale-selection- and fitting-convention-dependent estimates of variance and correlation scale.
claims.scope_conditions: Controlled supported Matérn alternatives and declared fitting losses; not a specimen result.
claims.unsupported_stronger_version: The synthetic parameter drift measures uncertainty in a real HgCdTe wafer.

claims.claim_text: Optical PSF, pixel or scan-bin integration, and depth weighting can materially change the transmitted variance and must remain explicit outside verified surrogate regimes.
claims.scope_conditions: Declared separable covariance and representative instrument stress grid.
claims.unsupported_stronger_version: A universal Gaussian PSF or rectangular pixel model applies to all instruments.

claims.claim_text: For a correlated finite raster, ordinary map variance can be biased relative to marginal filtered variance and nominal pixel count can greatly exceed effective information.
claims.scope_conditions: Exact Gaussian quadratic-form moments for declared covariance; effective degrees of freedom are moment matched.
claims.unsupported_stronger_version: Nominal map pixels are independent repeats or effective degrees of freedom follow an exact chi-square law generally.

claims.claim_text: Variance estimates at multiple scales obtained from one registered raster are correlated and require their full cross-scale covariance in parameter inference.
claims.scope_conditions: Exact under joint Gaussian map model; delta-method logarithmic covariance is approximate.
claims.unsupported_stronger_version: Numerically smoothed scales are independent measurements or model-conditioned covariance is empirical repeat covariance.

claims.claim_text: Parameter-efficient endpoint allocation and covariance-family falsification require different scale allocations, so an interior scale must be reserved when family testing is a design goal.
claims.scope_conditions: Declared local Gaussian Fisher design, scale interval, uncertainty model, and efficiency floor.
claims.unsupported_stronger_version: The reported allocation is a universal experimental prescription.

claims.claim_text: On the public CdSeTe PL peak-wavelength map, added numerical smoothing reduced apparent variance to 11.24 percent of its unsmoothed value at four pixels.
claims.scope_conditions: One 24 by 24 source-data-derived PL observation field with unknown native sample-plane kernel.
claims.unsupported_stronger_version: The result validates HgCdTe covariance or measures a physical CdSeTe correlation length.

claims.claim_text: The same CdSeTe map exhibits strong model-conditioned same-raster dependence, while boundary and crop choices change the variance curve more than the descriptive covariance-family separation.
claims.scope_conditions: Cross-scale covariance is conditional on a fitted descriptive Gaussian field; edge and crop results are finite-map sensitivities.
claims.unsupported_stronger_version: Matérn nu=3/2 is the material covariance law or the held-out numerical scale is independent validation.

claims.claim_text: No qualifying public source-native HgCdTe dataset was found for specimen-specific multiresolution validation under the declared public-only search.
claims.scope_conditions: Publicly indexed and audited sources only; does not prove private or unindexed data do not exist.
claims.unsupported_stronger_version: No suitable HgCdTe data exist anywhere.

claims.claim_text: The manuscript reports no specimen-specific HgCdTe point variance, physical correlation length, universal covariance family, or composition assignment from the CdSeTe PL field.
claims.scope_conditions: Applies to the entire manuscript and all figures, captions, abstract, and conclusions.
claims.unsupported_stronger_version: Descriptive pixel-space parameters or synthetic values are material constants.
